message:
I was digging through our hall closet last week looking for batteries—you know, the usual prep stuff—and found a stack of old board games. Haven't played those in *years*. So I grabbed one, dusted it off, and we ended up having the best family evening in months. No screens, no wifi needed, just actual laughs.

That's when it hit me: some of our best emergency-ready habits are *also* the ones that just make life nicer right now. A deck of cards, a flashlight you actually know how to use, paper and pens, a plan everyone knows—these things aren't just for "the bad day." They're kind of great anyway.

Made me rethink what preparedness really is. It's not just about having stuff. It's about knowing your people, having fun low-tech options on hand, and honestly? Being ready to handle life whether it's smooth or bumpy.

What's one simple thing you've kept around that turned out to be surprisingly useful *and* fun? 🕯️
